---
title: "Combined: Behavioral vs Prediction"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
          .g-col-3 { min-width: 0; }
          .g-col-3 .cell-output-display { overflow-x: hidden; }
          .g-col-3 .mosaic-widget label { flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch; min-height: unset; font-size: 0.85rem; }
          .g-col-3 .ts-wrapper { width: 100% !important; margin-left: 0 !important; margin-right: 0 !important; }
          .g-col-3 .ts-control { min-width: 0 !important; font-size: 0.85rem; }
          </style>
params:
  evals_path: "outputs/viz/default/evals_combined.parquet"
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
evals_path = "outputs/viz/default/evals_combined.parquet"
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

project_root = Path(".").resolve().parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))

def _resolve_path(p: str) -> str:
    pp = Path(p)
    if pp.is_absolute() and pp.exists():
        return str(pp)
    candidate = project_root / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    candidate = Path(".").resolve() / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    return p

evals_path_resolved = _resolve_path(evals_path)
_evals_df = pd.read_parquet(evals_path_resolved)
_out_dir = Path(evals_path_resolved).parent

# Drop rows with null prediction scores (can't plot those)
_evals_df = _evals_df.dropna(subset=["prediction_actual_score", "prediction_predicted_score"])

# Save filtered data for plots
_filtered_path = str(_out_dir / "_combined_filtered.parquet")
_evals_df.to_parquet(_filtered_path, index=False)

# N values are strings (converted in prepare_viz_data); sort numerically, keep as strings
n_domain = sorted(_evals_df["n_turns"].unique(), key=int)
max_n = n_domain[-1]

_label_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["n_turns"] == max_n].copy()
_label_path = str(_out_dir / "_combined_labels.parquet")
_label_df.to_parquet(_label_path, index=False)

_evals_df["calibration_error_pct"] = (_evals_df["prediction_predicted_score"] - _evals_df["behavioral_score"]) * 100
_evals_df["prediction_effect_pct"] = (_evals_df["prediction_actual_score"] - _evals_df["behavioral_score"]) * 100

_error_path = str(_out_dir / "_combined_errors.parquet")
_evals_df.to_parquet(_error_path, index=False)

from inspect_viz import Data, Selection
from inspect_viz.input import select
from inspect_viz.interactor import highlight, nearest_x
from inspect_viz.mark import line, text, cell
from inspect_viz.plot import plot

from scripts.viz_helpers import overview_heatmap
```

## Overview

Compares behavioral data (Protocol 1) with prediction data (Protocol 2) to answer:
1. **Prediction Calibration**: Does the model's prediction match its actual behavior?
2. **Effect of Prediction**: Does the act of predicting change the model's output?

```{python}
#| echo: false
line_data = Data.from_file(_filtered_path)
label_data = Data.from_file(_label_path)
error_data = Data.from_file(_error_path)

sel = Selection.intersect()
heatmap_sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### Filters

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Condition", column="condition", value="auto", target=sel, field="condition")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(error_data, label="Condition (heatmap)", column="condition", value="auto", target=heatmap_sel, field="condition")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Model", column="model", value="auto", target=sel, field="model")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Instruction", column="instruction", value="auto", target=sel, field="instruction")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(error_data, label="Instruction (heatmap)", column="instruction", value="auto", target=heatmap_sel, field="instruction")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
## Metric 1: Predicted vs Actual Behavior

Solid lines show actual instruction-following rate from Protocol 1 (behavioral baseline). Dashed lines show what the model *predicted* it would do in Protocol 2.

The gap reveals calibration error: does the model know its own behavior?

```{python}
#| echo: false
hover_sel = Selection.single(cross=True)
plot(
    line(
        line_data,
        x="n_turns",
        y="behavioral_score",
        stroke="model",
        filter_by=sel,
        marker=True,
        tip=True,
        channels={"N": "n_turns", "Actual (Protocol 1)": "behavioral_score", "Model": "model"},
    ),
    line(
        line_data,
        x="n_turns",
        y="prediction_predicted_score",
        stroke="model",
        stroke_dasharray="4 2",
        filter_by=sel,
        marker=True,
        tip=True,
        channels={"N": "n_turns", "Predicted (Protocol 2)": "prediction_predicted_score", "Model": "model"},
    ),
    text(
        label_data,
        x="n_turns",
        y="behavioral_score",
        text="model",
        filter_by=sel,
        fill="model",
        stroke_width=4,
        dx=5,
        line_anchor="middle",
        styles={"text_anchor": "start", "font_size": 11, "font_weight": 600, "stroke": "white"},
    ),
    nearest_x(hover_sel, fields=["model"]),
    highlight(by=hover_sel),
    x_label="N (hardcoded turns)",
    y_label="Instruction Following Rate",
    title="Actual (solid) vs Predicted (dashed) Behavior",
    width=900,
    height=400,
    y_domain=[0, 1.05],
    x_domain=n_domain,
    margin_right=200,
)
```

## Summary: Average Calibration Error

Average calibration error (predicted - actual) in percentage points, aggregated across all N values. Positive = model overestimates instruction-following; negative = underestimates.

### By Model (all conditions)

```{python}
#| echo: false
_evals_df.groupby("model")["calibration_error_pct"].mean().round(1).to_frame()
```

### instruction_hint

```{python}
#| echo: false
_hint_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["instruction"] == "instruction_hint"]
_hint_avg = _hint_df.groupby(["model", "condition"])["calibration_error_pct"].mean().round(1)
_hint_avg.unstack(level="model")
```

### instruction_no_hint

```{python}
#| echo: false
_nohint_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["instruction"] == "instruction_no_hint"]
_nohint_avg = _nohint_df.groupby(["model", "condition"])["calibration_error_pct"].mean().round(1)
_nohint_avg.unstack(level="model")
```

### Calibration Error Heatmap

Percentage points difference between predicted and actual behavior. Positive = model overestimates instruction-following; negative = underestimates.

```{python}
#| echo: false
_n_models = _evals_df["model"].nunique()
overview_heatmap(
    error_data,
    x="n_turns",
    y="model",
    fill_col="calibration_error_pct",
    selection=heatmap_sel,
    title="Calibration Error (Predicted - Actual) %",
    x_domain=n_domain,
    width=900,
    height=_n_models * 30 + 80,
)
```

## Metric 2: Effect of Prediction on Output

Compares actual output between protocols:
- **Solid**: Protocol 1 (behavioral) — model generates without prediction step
- **Dashed**: Protocol 2 (prediction) — model generates *after* making a prediction

Does asking the model to predict change what it outputs?

```{python}
#| echo: false
hover_sel2 = Selection.single(cross=True)
plot(
    line(
        line_data,
        x="n_turns",
        y="behavioral_score",
        stroke="model",
        filter_by=sel,
        marker=True,
        tip=True,
        channels={"N": "n_turns", "Protocol 1 (Behavioral)": "behavioral_score", "Model": "model"},
    ),
    line(
        line_data,
        x="n_turns",
        y="prediction_actual_score",
        stroke="model",
        stroke_dasharray="4 2",
        filter_by=sel,
        marker=True,
        tip=True,
        channels={"N": "n_turns", "Protocol 2 (After Prediction)": "prediction_actual_score", "Model": "model"},
    ),
    text(
        label_data,
        x="n_turns",
        y="behavioral_score",
        text="model",
        filter_by=sel,
        fill="model",
        stroke_width=4,
        dx=5,
        line_anchor="middle",
        styles={"text_anchor": "start", "font_size": 11, "font_weight": 600, "stroke": "white"},
    ),
    nearest_x(hover_sel2, fields=["model"]),
    highlight(by=hover_sel2),
    x_label="N (hardcoded turns)",
    y_label="Instruction Following Rate",
    title="Protocol 1 (solid) vs Protocol 2 After Prediction (dashed)",
    width=900,
    height=400,
    y_domain=[0, 1.05],
    x_domain=n_domain,
    margin_right=200,
)
```

### Prediction Effect Heatmap

Percentage points change in instruction-following rate after prediction step. Positive = prediction increases IF rate; negative = decreases.

```{python}
#| echo: false
overview_heatmap(
    error_data,
    x="n_turns",
    y="model",
    fill_col="prediction_effect_pct",
    selection=heatmap_sel,
    title="Effect of Prediction (Protocol 2 - Protocol 1) %",
    x_domain=n_domain,
    width=900,
    height=_n_models * 30 + 80,
)
```
:::

::::
